description: Patient John Zenon, a maintenance worker at an industrial cold-storage facility, was accidentally locked in a -25°C blast freezer. He presents with violent shivering, numbness in fingers, and core body temperature of 35.2°C (mild hypothermia). As the attending occupational health physician, document this acute exposure in GNU Health: document the cold stress diagnosis (T68 Hypothermia or T33 Superficial frostbite), create a clinical evaluation recording his hypothermic temperature (≤ 35.5 °C), order at least 2 baseline laboratory tests for tissue damage (e.g., CPK, CMP, CBC), prescribe an analgesic for rewarming pain (e.g., Ibuprofen, Ketorolac, Acetaminophen, or Aspirin), and schedule a tissue viability follow-up appointment for 2 to 7 days from today. Login credentials: Username: admin, Password: gnusolidario.
